Hello From France, 
Ayant quelques bases, notion en vfx, 3D...Je le recommande fortement (vous pouvez tout comprendre dès le générique) mais on ne voit pas le temps passer. Je reste mitigé face aux critiques que j'ai pu voir. La 2nde scène d'action est to much (un peu foireuse), sinon la suite est vraiment niveau 4,2/5. Ce n'est pas le meilleur James Bond pour une simple raison, le film est très sympa à voir une fois mais beaucoup de scènes pourront vous sembler longue si vous les voyez 10 fois. En termes émouvants... on part sur du 5/5. En terme de dialogue il est sûr qu'il y a mieux mais franchement allons nous voir un James Bond pour les dialogues ou pour l'action ? Alors ne vous poser pas de question, les notes ne sont que des chiffres, foncer !

What is special in Bond movies.
1. Opening scene 
2.Thrilling edge of seats stunts
3. Villain's henchmen
4. Villain
5. Casinos game play scripts.
6. High quality car chase.
7. Climax
How to rate No Time TO Die
1. Opening scene.  - There is no such opening scene
2.Thrilling edge of seats stunts - Hardly any
3. Villain's henchmen - Lets not talk about it.
4. Villain - If I find it I will surely reveal.
5. Casinos game play scripts. - None
6. High quality car chase. - Petty scenes, cannot be called it complete car chase.
7. Climax - Third rate filthy and worst climax, all of a sudden henchmen appear, when director needs henchmen will  disappear, Unrealistic climax, better to say unconvincing way of writing a climax. 
Overall the worst bond movie, only good point is daniel craig, else the movie is waste, utter waste, a third rate action movie is far better than NO TIME TO DIE, It is better to label as  THIS MOVIE'S TIME TO DIE

When I was a kid it was Timmothy Dalton….   These movies were all incredible, no question.  Much more “serious” than Ian Flemming intended I believe. Daniel Craig didn’t have a ton of gadgets and items to help him along his missions.  Signature gun, antidote/AED, the little explosive keychain in CR. Much more realistic instead of a crazy adventure story about a secret agent who has jetpacks, mini helicopters with all types of guns/aerial bombs, ejector seats, oil slick in tail lights and pens with poison in them. Trap doors with sharks/Pirhanhas waiting. Clearly has gadgets in the DB5 this go around. Regardless,  sad to see Craig retire the part he was absolutely amazing for sure.

The last and by far worst edition in the Craig 007-series... After the initial 20 minutes, at moments the film is slow and dare I say it... plain boring. The first 20 minutes are great. Then James drops Madelaine off at the train and the film sinks and never really recovers. Dialogues are erratic, the storyline inconsistent and Craig tries to give Bond layers but becomes a parody of himself. The story of the bad gay had no depth and thus he is totally unimpressive. The acting of the guy was great but his character is not well developped. What you end up with is a disappointing 165 minutes.  The #metoo movement sure has made it's mark... on a scale of 1-10 I give the movie a 5.

Here's a perspective from someone who is more "right" leaning, doesn't go in for all the woke nonsense, but generally keeps a level head.

So, you guys think that because a black woman features prominently in the promotional material, and has a major role in the film, that it somehow means that she's going to take the lead role in future installments? Allow me to give you all a little history lesson. Grace Jones featured heavily in the promotional material for A View to a Kill (Sir Roger Moore's final outing) and played a major role in the movie. Does that mean that the original plan for was for her (and I use that term lightly) to take over in The Living Daylights?

NONE of us know where it's heading next; the same way that we didn't know where the  franchise was heading next at the end of every other Bonds tenure. If you people weren't so mentally fragile and so emotionally attached to works of fiction you'd actually remember that. The fact that this iteration of Bond went out in a different way doesn't necessarily mean the next iteration is going to be drastically different.

Stop letting your emotions overrule logic.

The younger audience is mostly going to be enamored by Daniel Craig & Pierce Brosnan and die-hard Bond fans will obviously rank Sean Connery, Pierce Brosnan, and Daniel Craig as the best Bonds. However, I also think that THE LIVING DAYLIGHTS is one of the best Bond movies ever (in my top 3) with a memorable performance by Timothy Dalton.

The best thing for the Bond franchise would be to end here. Bond has no future - corporate conglomerates control the world now. Much of the warfare has now shifted online and/or pertains to financial skullduggery which is beyond Bond’s old school expertise that mostly targeted the low hanging fruit and other pawns in the game, rather than addressing root causes of problems - the very countries and companies that Bond is acting on behalf of. It'd be nice to see Bond take on the financial services industry for instance. The focus has shifted to issues impacting humanity and blind adherence to nationalism is not something that flies with moviegoers anymore. The days of a world famous, bourgeois spy, reveling in the company of women and fine dining, are over.

Just watched it.. it's a huge letdown imo. Craig's arc ended in a whimper, his valiant effort brought down by a shoddy plot and a shockingly generic villain that of all things reminded me of the Brosnan era. What little emotional and human baggage it tried to impart to Bond, the film threw around clumsily, and the ending left hardly an impression. 

i mean, I'm sure there'll be many who love it and you should still definitely check it out, for Craig's sake if nothing else... but this is probably his weakest outing.

so overall, Craig's Bond films from best to worst:
1. Skyfall. The internet knows why, the critics know why and you know why. Nuff said. 
2. Casino Royale: A very well done reboot that effectively sets the tone for a truly modern Bond arc. Plenty of wit, action and a surprisingly heartfelt romance make it a still highly watchable film after all these years. 
3. Quantum of Solace: Probably debatable but to me Quantum is vastly underrated. Alot of people talk about how the writer strike ruined the film but it's a tale of revenge and salvation that actually grows on you. If you care to pay attention, this film shows Bond's transformation from a rage-filled killer to a truly mature 007. It's a pivotal film to the development of Craig's Bond.
4. Spectre: I don't quite like this honestly, but it faced an impossibly high benchmark after Skyfall. It felt bloated and at times unnecessary, without the fine touches and nuances that defined its predecessor. But I applaud Mendes' effort in myth/world building. 
5. No Time to Die.

Gravity Slave while I disagree on the diversity statement, I agree with not changing the source material. 

Diversity can be a positive effect because it gives minorities of different racial, religious, and sometimes even different gender backgrounds a chance to have their voice heard and their people represented. Cause throughout most of history, white people have dominated the majority of film and TV. So when a film like In the Heights or Crazy Rich Asians comes around, it’s special because it shines the spotlight on overlooked social groups and societies. 

However, I do agree you shouldn’t change the existing source material for the sake of diversity. Diversity is best used when it’s allowed to have characters that stand on their own and not just a rehash of older stuff. 

Let’s take a look at 2 examples of this: a Black Spider-Man and a Black Superman. Miles Morales works on his own because he’s not just a Black/Puerto-Rican rehash of Peter Parker, but rather his own character (hate when people compare him to Peter like he doesn’t have to be compared to a white character to have any significance. He’s stands perfectly fine on his own feet). What WB wanted to do with making Clark Kent Black though is a different story. It completely takes away from the Superman character as we know him. An icon that’s been represented for years that everyone accepts. Changing his race would just push a social agenda that’s not suited for that type of story.

Gravity Slave that’s the gist of what I’m trying to say. You shouldn’t ruin the source material for wanting diversity. They should make their own characters if they want it so bad.

Though Idk how Spider-Man comes into this. Like I said, Miles is his own character separate from Peter Parker in the multiverse. Making a Black Clark Kent, however, is wrong and completely changes the character of Superman for the sake of diversity

I thought Moore was very good in Live And Let Die and in The Man With The Golden Gun.
He was good for toning down Bond a little post-Sean Connery in terms of aggression when movies were becoming too violent from the late 1960s onwards. Plus the humour element was a trait that appealed to a wider audience, worldwide at the time. (I can recall young teenagers watching The Spy Who Loved Me at the cinema in early 1978 when I watched it). After Craig has gone, as brilliantly as he has re-injected menace back into Bond’s character, I hope the next Bond can channel some of Roger Moore’s tongue-in-cheek humour, subtly so, because …. because if the next big Bond villain is going to be a potentially very politically incorrect choice, namely someone who can believably be depicted as an enemy of the West (Bond is good when he’s got his priorities right!), then to smooth out the eventual release of THAT film, a return to the sardonic humour of Moore would gratify and amuse a worldwide audience much more successfully than a Craig copy.  There is a way to ease tension AND hold a mirror up to ourselves. This is more important than you think, as the entertainments are dripping in the projection of petty little pious poses that just do not reflect the characters, the make-up, within all of us.

Fantastic trailer and i'm sure a great conclusion to the Craig Bond series that I truly love. Casino Royale was the start of a new area of Bond. I grew up with Bond, starting with Connery. Times change, movies change. All the Daniel Craig Bond films are excellent, no question. The only reasonable argument I consider, is who was the best Bond. I have to start with Connery, because I grew up with him and original is always best. Second is George Lazenby. You can say what you want, but he was a man. Third is Craig. I've read every single Bond book by Ian Fleming and Connery, Lazenby and Craig come closed to Bond. Craig is number one for me if I look at Bond as a new chapter, for a new time. 4th is Dalton, 5th place is shared by Brosnan and Moore. Too handsome, too smooth, too plastic. Let us not forget, Bond is suppose to be an ice cold killer. ...and since we are on topic, next Bond.. my choice is Toby Stephens. Anyone who has seen Black Sails will agree that he has the looks and the intensity that is needed. If Bond turns black or female the end is near. Not saying this with any prejudice, but some things should just be left alone. Seriously....don't appropriate the Bond name and character, just create a spin off character like 008 or what ever and run with it. You'll have my full support as a movie fan.

I'm a big James Bond fan, but I'm sick of hearing about this movie!  Perhaps it would have been better if they had just streamed it!  This one has been years in the making, yet its running time is in the two hour forty five minute range.  With the delays due to the virus, the filmmakers have had months to cut this picture down to a reasonable length.  I'm sorry, but a good Bond picture does not need to be this long.  For a prime example of how a long running time can kill a good picture at the box office, just ask the makers of Blade Runner: 2049.  Now that indoor masks are mandated yet again in our city, I'll be damned if I am going to wear a stupid mask inside a crowded, stuffy movie theater, just so the makers of this picture can try to set some kind of a box office record.  Another thing: I Love the Craig Bond pictures, but I hope that future Bond pictures will restore some of carefree fun that classic pictures like Goldfinger had.

I remember my parents buying me the VHS box sets for the original Bonds when I was younger and obsessing over them. Every time a new Bond came out, my dad and I would get tickets and go to the theatre together, it's still something we look forward to. When Craig was announced as the new Bond, I remember so many critics hating the idea almost writing the series off. 15 years later, the decision to cast Daniel Craig as Bond, in my opinion, holds up as the best direction the series has ever taken. The original Bonds were mostly quirky, fun and a young boys fantasy but Daniel Craig's Bond has emotion, he has grit, he's human. The fun gadgets and espionage that helped me fall in love with the series are still there but in a more reasonable dosage and Craig, as this more grounded Bond, continually keeps me engaged.

Thank you Mr. Craig for not only taking care of this series but moving it to the next level, setting a high standard for the franchise's future. I cannot wait for No Time To Die!

Odin is also known as Wotan. He can also be called Watan. Digamma, waw, or wau (uppercase: Ϝ, lowercase: ϝ, numeral: ϛ) is an archaic letter of the Greek alphabet. It originally stood for the sound /w/ but it has remained in use principally as a Greek numeral for 6. The shape of the letter went through a development from f to s. Also ç is similar to s. Odin is the ruler of the runes. The rune Fehu derived from the waw letter. F is also spelled v.
So we have Odin > Watan > Vatan > 𝙎at𝘼n.

Oannes is a god from Babylon, the counterpart of the Sumerian Enki. His greek counterpart is Ioannes, the ancient name of Dionysus, the god of wine. Enki is the god of water. This is an allegory of the Ambrosia, secreted by the pineal gland that heightens the consciousness. Also the great flood of the Sumerian myth is an allegory of the Nigredo that washes the dirt away from the soul. The symbol of Dionysus is the peacock. The supreme god of the 𝙔e𝙕i𝘿i𝙎 is 𝙈e𝙇e𝙆 𝙏au𝙎 (peacock). All of them are the same god. The 𝙔e𝙕i𝘿i𝙎 descend from Persians that descend from Sumerians, and are known to worship the 𝘿e𝙑il, 𝙎at𝘼n.
